sections.heading: Subclass 500 in Schedule 2
sections.text: The Migration Regulations 1994 include Subclass 500 (Student) visa provisions in Schedule 2. This means the legal criteria for grant of a Student visa are set out in the regulations rather than only in general guidance.

sections.heading: Student visa criteria and evidence context
sections.text: For Subclass 500 matters, the regulations operate together with legislative instruments and departmental guidance that deal with issues such as English-language evidence, enrolment-related matters, and other visa criteria. In practice, a refusal analysis should check the exact criterion or requirement relied on in the decision.

sections.heading: Why the exact refusal ground matters
sections.text: A Subclass 500 refusal should be analysed by identifying the specific criterion, legislative instrument, or factual concern relied on by the decision-maker. General information about review rights may not be enough without the refusal notice and the exact basis for refusal.
